headlines.title: Asian shares are mostly higher and Mideast tensions push Brent crude past $96
Nikkei 225 Index wavers as Japan bond yields and tech stocks rise
Japanese Yen firms on intervention risks, eyes FOMC and BoJ meetings
BOJ to raise rates again by December as weak yen revives inflation risks: Reuters poll
Japan's Nikkei rises as chip shares gain, BOJ's rate hike prospects weigh
Japan's Nikkei rises as chip shares gain, BOJ's rate hike prospects weigh
Japan's PayPay, Seven & i to combine loyalty programs

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 오늘 반도체 및 기술주 강세에 힘입어 닛케이 225 지수가 상승세를 보이며 혼조 양상을 나타냈습니다. 그러나 일본 국채 금리 상승과 엔화 약세로 인한 인플레이션 재점화 우려로 일본은행(BOJ)의 추가 금리 인상 전망이 시장 상단을 제한했습니다. 엔화는 외환시장 개입 경계감 속에 강세를 보였으며, 투자자들은 향후 정책 방향을 가늠하기 위해 미국 FOMC 및 BOJ 회의 결과에 주목하고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market saw a mixed performance today, with the Nikkei 225 Index generally rising, primarily driven by gains in chip and tech shares. However, the market's upside was tempered by rising Japan bond yields and ongoing prospects of further Bank of Japan rate hikes, fueled by a weak yen and revived inflation risks. The Japanese Yen firmed amidst intervention risks, with investors closely watching upcoming FOMC and BOJ meetings for future policy direction.
